zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Thomas Gerard於1431年7月15日出生在英國藍卡爾布萊恩時，他的父親Sir Peter Gerard of Bryn24歲，母親Isabel Strangways19歲。他於大約1440年與Lady Douce Ashton在英國英格蘭藍卡爾阿希頓-德-林結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1490年3月27日在他的家鄉享年58歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭藍卡爾文維其可。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Thomas Gerard于1431年7月15日出生在英国藍卡爾布萊恩时，他的父亲Sir Peter Gerard of Bryn24岁，母亲Isabel Strangways19岁。他于大约1440年与Lady Douce Ashton在英国英格兰藍卡爾阿希頓-德-林结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1490年3月27日在他的家乡享年58岁，埋葬在英国英格兰藍卡爾文維其可。

nameMeanings.name: Gerard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Camille, Jean-Pierre, Murielle, Emile, Pascal, Pierre, Agathe, Alain, Aldes, Antoine, Armand.

French (Gérard), Walloon (mainly Gérard), and English (of Norman origin): from the personal name Gérard, Gerard (from Old French Gerart, Girart), composed of the ancient Germanic elements gēr ‘spear, lance’ + hard ‘hardy, brave, strong’. As a surname of French origin it is also found in Haiti. Compare Garrard and also French Guerard , English Garard , Gerrard , and Jarrard .

variants.name: Gerardi
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Vito, Enzo, Gaspare, Luigi, Rocco, Antonio, Canio, Carmine, Filippo, Francesco, Giovanni, Libero.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of the ancient Germanic personal name Gerardo (see Gerard ). This surname is also found in southern France.
German and French (mainly Nord): Latinized (humanistic) patronymic from the personal name German Gerhard , French Gérard (see Gerard ), using the genitive of the Latinized form Gerardus.

variants.name: Garrison
meanings: English: from a shortened form of Middle English Gerardson, a patronymic from the personal name Gerard + son, or an Anglicized form of its Dutch cognate Gerritsen . The English surname is probably in most cases of Flemish or Dutch origin. It was brought to Britain by immigrants from the Low Countries, especially in the 15th and 16th centuries.
Americanized form of Jewish   Gerson   (see   Gershon  ).
Americanized form of Dutch   Gerritsen  .

variants.name: Garrard
meanings: English (London and East Anglia): variant of   Gerard  .
Altered form of French Gérard (see Gerard ).
History: This surname (see 2 above) is listed in the register of Huguenot ancestors recognized by the Huguenot Society of South Carolina.

variants.name: Geenen
meanings: Dutch, Flemish, and North German: patronymic from the personal name Geen, a pet form of Gerard .

variants.name: Gerardo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Francisco, Alberto, Alfredo, Aurelio, Carlos, Jesus, Juan, Norberto, Ramon, Tomas, Ana.  Italian Antonio, Carmine, Dino, Enrico, Marco, Marco Antonio.
Spanish and Italian: from the personal name Gerardo (see Gerard ).

variants.name: Gerald
meanings: English (of Norman origin); French (Gérald): from the ancient Germanic personal name Gerald, Girald, composed of the elements gēr ‘spear, lance’ and wald ‘power, authority’. It was introduced to Britain by the Normans and was fairly common, but less so than Gerard , with which it was early confused. In France the much more common variants of this surname are Géraud and Giraud .

variants.name: Gerrard
meanings: English: variant of   Gerard  .

variants.name: Geron
meanings: French (Géron): from the personal name Gero, Gerone, a short form of ancient Germanic names formed with the element gār, gēr ‘spear, lance’, as for example Gérard (see Gerard ).
Altered form of French Guérin (see Guerin ).
English (Lancashire): of Norman origin, from the personal name Geron of ancient Germanic origin, a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Gherardi
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Silvio, Alessandro, Angelo, Antonio, Donato, Egidio, Ennio, Enzo, Gildo, Lucio, Luigi, Primo.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of Gherardo, one of themany Italian equivalents of Gerard .

variants.name: Garard
meanings: English (London and Buckinghamshire): variant of   Gerard  .

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Thomas Gerard was born on 15 July 1431, in Bryn, Lancashire, England, his father, Sir Peter Gerard of Bryn, was 24 and his mother, Isabel Strangways, was 19. He married Lady Douce Ashton about 1440, in Ashton-under-Lyne, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 27 March 1490, in his hometown, at the age of 58, and was buried in Winwick, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Thomas Gerard est né le 15 juillet 1431, à Bryn, Lancashire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Peter Gerard of Bryn, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Isabel Strangways, avait 19 ans. Il a épousé Lady Douce Ashton environ 1440, à Ashton-under-Lyne, Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 27 mars 1490, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 58 ans, et a été enterré à Winwick, Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

Er hat um 1440 in Ashton-under-Lyne, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom Lady Douce Ashton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 27. Lui sposato Lady Douce Ashton circa 1440, nel Ashton-under-Lyne, Lancashire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 2 figlie. ko.lifeSummary: Sir Thomas Gerard(이)가 1431년 7월 15일 영국 랭카실르 브린에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Peter Gerard of Bryn(은)는 24세였고, 그의 어머니 Isabel Strangways(은)는 19세였다. 그는 대략 1440년 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 애슈토-운더-리니에서 Lady Douce Ashton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1490년 3월 27일 그의 고향에서 58세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 윈윅에 묻혔다.

pt.Ashton in Makerfield, Lancashire, Englan: Ashton in Makerfield, Lancashire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Thomas Gerard nasceu em 15 de julho de 1431, em Bryn, Lancashire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Peter Gerard of Bryn, tinha 24 anos e sua mãe, Isabel Strangways, tinha 19 anos. Ele casou-se com Lady Douce Ashton aproximadamente 1440, em Ashton-under-Lyne, Lancashire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 27 de março de 1490, em sua cidade natal, com 58 anos, e foi sepultado em Winwick, Lancashire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.Ashton in Makerfield, Lancashire, Englan: аштон ин меикирфиелд, ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Thomas Gerard родился 15 июля 1431 г. в местности брин, ланкашир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Peter Gerard of Bryn было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Isabel Strangways, 19. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Douce Ashton около 1440 г. в местности аштон-ундир-лин, ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 2 дочери. Он умер 27 марта 1490 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 58 лет и был похоронен в местности винвикк, ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Thomas Gerard nació el 15 de julio de 1431, en Bryn, Lancashire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Peter Gerard of Bryn, tenía 24 años y su mamá, Isabel Strangways, tenía 19 años. Se casó con Lady Douce Ashton aproximadamente 1440, en Ashton-under-Lyne, Lancashire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 27 de marzo de 1490, en su pueblo, a la edad de 58 años, y fue sepultado en Winwick, Lancashire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
